select_when: A failure is real but the cause is ambiguous, distributed, or hidden behind several interacting components.
The team can form explicit hypotheses, isolate variables, and test the system instead of arguing from intuition.
Reliability matters enough that vague explanations are more dangerous than the time required to trace the true defect path.
The work is conflating symptom, diagnosis, and solution design, so it needs a disciplined root-cause pass before action.
avoid_when: People have not agreed on the actual failure condition, so debugging would optimize noise, symptoms, or tools instead of broken behavior.
The problem is genuinely novel and needs abductive or creative generation before deductive debugging frameworks can help.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model focused on root-cause diagnosis, variable isolation, and disciplined sequencing rather than broad software-only debugging language.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor troubleshooting sprawl because the raw source is already strong on diagnosis, issue trees, and anti-premature-solution discipline.
open_questions: The source spans business problem solving and technical diagnosis, so the typed output compresses several execution surfaces.

select_when.note: The raw source explicitly names ambiguous failures, hypothesis testing, reliability-sensitive diagnosis, and step-order discipline.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions come directly from the source warnings about undefined failure conditions and novel problems that need different reasoning.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates around diagnosis under ambiguity, while the typed label is reviewed operational normalization.

output_type.note: The source yields root-cause tracing and test sequencing; the typed output label is a compact reviewed abstraction.

reasoning_types.note: Debugging is explicitly about causal diagnosis and root-cause analysis, but the taxonomy labels remain normalized.
